name: Oscos, Navia Basin, and Sierra de Penouta Route
name_original: Cimadevila, Pozo de la Nieve y Alto de Penouta – circular desde Boal por la Sierra de Bobia
description: This difficult 33.2-mile road cycling route through Asturias offers challenging climbs and panoramic views from Alto de Penouta.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Boal traverse a diverse landscape characterized by deep valleys and mountainous terrain, including the Penouta and La Bobia mountain ranges. The region features verdant chestnut and birch forests, lush meadows, and the significant Navia River basin. Road cyclists can expect quiet, twisting roads and challenging climbs, often with panoramic views of the valley and the Asturian coast. Best road cycling routes around Boal The most popular road cycling route is Oscos, Navia Basin, and Sierra de Penouta Route, a 33.2 miles (53.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive climbs through the Navia River basin and the Sierra de Penouta. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop, a difficult 33.3 miles (53.6 km) path. It features significant elevation gain as it winds through the La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta mountain ranges. Local road cyclists also love the Luarca Lighthouse – Navia loop from Navia, a 68.2 miles (109.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas and river valleys, often completed in about 6 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Boal is defined by deep river valleys, challenging mountain passes, and scenic coastal sections. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Boal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from around 20 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Boal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Boal?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Boal, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from nearly 20 reviews.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Boal?
A: Road cycling in Boal is characterized by diverse terrain, including deep river valleys, challenging mountain passes like the Penouta and La Bobia ranges, and scenic coastal sections. You'll find quiet, twisting roads, often with significant elevation changes, and panoramic views of the valley and the Asturian coast.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Boal?
A: Yes, while many routes feature challenging climbs, Boal does offer options for less experienced cyclists. There are 2 easy and 15 moderate routes available, providing a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes. These routes often utilize mostly paved surfaces, making them accessible for various ability levels.

Q: Which routes offer the most challenging climbs for advanced cyclists?
A: For advanced cyclists seeking significant elevation gain, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077406285/oscos-navia-basin-and-sierra-de-penouta-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oscos, Navia Basin, and Sierra de Penouta Route&lt;/a&gt; (53.4 km with 1,461 m elevation gain) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353795558/la-bobia-and-sierra-de-penouta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop&lt;/a&gt; (53.6 km with 1,465 m elevation gain) provide extensive climbs through mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Boal?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Boal are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353795558/la-bobia-and-sierra-de-penouta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option. The region also features routes like the Senda Costa Naviega Loop, a moderate coastal circular route.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I find along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers stunning panoramic views, particularly from the Penouta and La Bobia mountain ranges. Viewpoints like the one at Penouta, situated at approximately 800 meters, provide spectacular vistas of the valley and the Asturian coast. The Navia River basin also offers picturesque scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in Boal?
A: While cycling, you can explore areas characterized by chestnut and birch forests, lush meadows, and the significant Navia River basin. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2540246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oneta Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are a notable natural highlight. You might also encounter impressive canyons along the riverbanks.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. You can find well-preserved Celtic settlements like "Castro de Pendia" and ancient burial mounds in the Penouta mountain range. Towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navia&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5562456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caridad&lt;/a&gt; offer glimpses into local culture and history.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Boal?
A: The region's challenging mountain passes, often featured in the Vuelta a España, suggest that spring, summer, and early autumn offer the most favorable conditions for road cycling. During these seasons, the weather is generally more stable and conducive to tackling the varied terrain.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Boal?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from deep valleys and mountainous terrain to verdant forests and coastal sections. The challenging climbs and panoramic views are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes for endurance training?
A: Absolutely. For longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca Lighthouse – Navia loop from Navia&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 109.7 km with over 2,500 m of elevation gain, typically taking around 6 hours to complete. Other extensive options include the 107 km Porcía Beach – Luarca Fishing Village loop (1,170m elevation gain) and the 162 km Luarca Fishing Village – Ramón de Campoamor Park loop (2,030m elevation gain) originating near Navia.

Q: Can I find places to rest or refuel along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes aren't listed for every route, many routes pass through or near charming fishing villages and towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navia&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5562456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caridad&lt;/a&gt;. These settlements typically offer opportunities for refreshments and breaks.
tips: Beautiful picture that we found rolling through the Sierra de San Isidro between Cimadevila and the top of Pozo de la Nieve. A marvel for road bike lovers.

Lovely ride through the crest of la Bobia with stunning views down different valleys.

Especially recommended when you are riding it downwards, as you'll be still fast even when wind works against you (much better riding against the wind here than in the flat!).

El Alto de Penouta is another classic from western Asturias. We can choose between several variants, on its north and west faces to climb up to an altitude of 780 meters above sea level. From the top we can see the coast and the distant town of Tapia de Casariego and/or the spectacular valley of the Navia river.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route is approximately 53.4 kilometers long. Given its difficult rating and significant elevation gain, it typically takes around 3 hours and 15 minutes of active cycling time to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and stops.

faqs.question: Is the Oscos, Navia Basin, and Sierra de Penouta Route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners. It features significant climbs, including a demanding ascent of Sierra de Penouta with an average gradient of 6.1%. It requires good fitness and experience with mountainous terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mountainous and demanding, characterized by rugged, pine-covered mountains and deep ravines. You'll experience significant climbs, particularly within the Sierra de Penouta, and traverse lush river valleys in the Navia Basin. Expect winding roads and panoramic views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable points. These include the scenic Winding Road in Sierra de San Isidro, the impressive La Bobia Ridge, and the summit of Alto de Penouta, which offers expansive views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The beautiful Asturias region is ideal for cycling in milder weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with moderate temperatures and less extreme weather, making them the best times to tackle this challenging route.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Oscos, Navia Basin, and Sierra de Penouta Route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the starting point are not provided, routes in this region typically begin from towns or villages where public parking is available. It's advisable to check local parking options in Boal or nearby settlements before your ride.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public areas, it's not typically considered a 'dog-friendly' activity in the same way a hiking trail might be. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely accompany you on a bike or is secured in a trailer, and always follow local leash laws.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially within the Río Eo, Oscos y Terras de Burón Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Río Eo, Oscos y Terras de Burón Biosphere Reserve. Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling on public roads within the reserve. However, always respect local regulations, especially regarding protected areas and private property.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other notable cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the 'Road Oscos, Cuenca del Navia y Sierra de Penouta' (46%) and also includes parts of '[CIMA AS32] La Bobia * Puente Urubio / Doiras' (40%). Smaller segments of other trails like 'Ruta del puente colgante' and various '[CIMA AS32] La Bobia' variations are also part of the experience.

faqs.question: Are there any services like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: The region is popular for cycle tourism, so you can expect to find services in the towns and villages along or near the route. Look for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in places like Boal and other settlements within the Oscos and Navia Basin areas to refuel and rest.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific route is described as a circular tour starting and ending from Boal, making it convenient for planning your ride without needing a return trip arrangement.

waypoints.name: Winding Road in Sierra de San Isidro
